topics.title: Central Europe swelters under record heat
topics.summary: A severe heatwave is pushing Central Europe toward record temperatures, buckling German highways, straining rail travel and keeping heat alerts in place across several countries.
topics.discussionPrompt: When weather gets hot enough to bend roads and disrupt trains, does it change how you think about summer travel plans?

topics.title: Trump warns Europe over digital taxes
topics.summary: President Donald Trump threatened 100% tariffs on countries that impose digital services taxes on US tech firms, drawing pushback from the European Commission.
topics.discussionPrompt: Tech companies are global, but taxes are national. Who do you think should have the bigger say: countries, companies, or trade partners?

topics.title: World Cup piracy sites seized
topics.summary: US authorities seized nearly 400 domains accused of illegally streaming World Cup matches, saying the sites also created cybersecurity risks for viewers.
topics.discussionPrompt: Streaming sports can get complicated fast. Do crackdowns like this protect fans, or mostly remind people how fragmented viewing has become?

topics.title: Uruguay exits World Cup without a win
topics.summary: Uruguay's World Cup run ended with no wins after a 1-0 loss to Spain, raising questions about coach Marcelo Bielsa's future with the team.
topics.discussionPrompt: When a famous sports team underperforms, do you blame the coach, the players, bad luck, or just the pressure of the tournament?
